page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or a GPU, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.re6p.large demonstrates modest processing capabilities. Its stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1312.39 falls into the bottom 25%, while its multi-core performance score of 1746.48 is in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show 97.49 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, but uncached large-block reads and writes drop to 14.09 GB/sec and 12.03 GB/sec respectively, both placing in the bottom 10%. PassMark CPU and memory scores also reflect lower-tier performance, with database operations scoring 729.61 and Redis SET operations reaching 1.69 million operations per second. However, single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves an average tier score of 8.99 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but lower-tier benchmark results, this instance offers an entry-level hardware profile. It is best suited for lightweight, non-intensive workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high memory bandwidth or rapid storage I/O. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, lightweight background tasks, simple single-threaded applications, and development or testing environments.

It includes 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU resources. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU scores placing in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, and memory bandwidth showing similar lower-tier results. Despite dedicated CPU allocation, its resource density and processing limits suggest a qualitative focus on entry-level workloads. The instance is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, and development environments that do not require high-performance computing or intensive memory throughput.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with balanced memory configuration for entry-level computing and lightweight application hosting.
